title: Plate Reader experiment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a7nf1","text":"This protocol covers the inoculation, cultivation, IPTG-induction and plate reader measurement of E. coli BL21 strains carrying constructs with various toehold switch devices from Green et al., 2014.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":98,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":186,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b9neu","text":"The class will divide into four groups; each group will investigate one separate RNA device from Green et al. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":103,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8q785","text":"Each device consists of two plasmids - a switch plasmid containing GFPmut3b, cis-repressed by a synthetic regulatory 5'UTR (untranslated region), denoted by H-xx-GFP, and a trigger plasmid containing a small regulatory RNA, denoted by T-xx, which binds to the switch sequence and switches on translation of GFP.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"r8mc","text":"The following table shows the strains used by each group:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"58tfh","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9o50m","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"59s3","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","0_2":"","0_3":"","0_4":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","1_3":"","1_4":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","2_2":"","2_3":"","2_4":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","3_2":"","3_3":"","3_4":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","4_2":"","4_3":"","4_4":""},"colWidths":[130,130,130,154,10],"data":[["\u0026nbsp;","Toehold switch device","Switch only control","non-specific trigger control","\u0026nbsp;"],["Group 1\n","\u0026nbsp; T56, H56-GFP","\u0026nbsp;H56-GFP","\u0026nbsp; T117, H56-GFP","\u0026nbsp;"],["Group 2","\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;T69, H69-GFP","\u0026nbsp;H69-GFP","\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp; T56, H69-GFP","\u0026nbsp;"],["Group 3","\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;T96, H96-GFP","\u0026nbsp;H96-GFP","\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp; steps: Inoculate 5 mL preculture of each strain using appropriate antibiotics. H-xx switch plasmids: Kanamycin (final concentration 25 µg/mL) T-xx trigger plasmids: Ampicillin (final concentration 100 µg/mL) Incubate in a shaking incubator over night at 37˚C.

Early morning: From each preculture, prepare a main culture in Erlenmeyer flasks. Dilute the preculture 1:100 in a final volume of 20 mL LB (again using appropriate antibiotics). Grow at 37˚C to an OD 600 of 0.4-0.6 (start measuring OD 600 after ≈ 1.5 hours). Optional: If some cultures grow faster than others, flasks can be kept on ice to stop growth. 01:30:00 While measuring the ODs, the flasks should only be taken out for sampling and immediately returned to the incubator, otherwise growth will slow down!

Morning: Each culture should be measured in duplicates, growing one sample with H 2 0 (-) and one with IPTG (+) - resulting in 12 samples per group. Induction experiments are performed in 12-well plates. In a final volume of 2 mL per well, supplement with IPTG (final concentration 100 µM) or sterile H 2 O. Incubate plates in a shaking incubator for three hours. a 03:00:00

While cells are incubating, prepare a pipetting scheme for your plate reader measurements. Two groups will share one 96-well plate. Each culture should be pipetted in at least three technical replicates. Pipet 75 µL LB and 75 µL culture per well. While one person measures the cultures at the plate reader, the rest of the group should start RNA preparation (label tubes, cool centrifuge, prepare fume hood). After pooling the duplicates, use 1 mL of E. coli culture for RNA extraction.
creator: Anna Behle
